posts.text:
Whether you support the war with Iran or not, let’s be honest about one thing:

No one knows how this ends.

No expert.
No analyst.
No politician.

I know this personally.

I lived in Tehran during the Iran–Iraq war (1980–1988). Eight years of uncertainty. Eight years of fear.

There’s a sound from that time that has never left my mind.

A voice over the loudspeaker saying:

“Tavajoh, tavajoh, alāmate ghermez.”
توجه توجه، علامت قرمز

Attention. Attention. Red alert. 

Signaling that enemy aircra

Now they want to talk?


Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (@RepLuna)

Update translation: Talks (negotiations) will begin shortly. 

"They want to talk, and I have agreed to talk, so I will be talking to them. They should have done it sooner. They should have given what was very practical and easy to do sooner. They waited too long," Trump said in the interview from his Florida residence.

posts.text:
81% Iranians want a regime change.

Nationwide survey conducted by GAMAAN covering about 158,000 Iranians inside the country and 42,000 abroad. 

This number has hovered between 75-81% the last 3-5 years.

Video

posts.text:
Today we lost Robert Duvall, one of the greatest actors of our generation.

The only movie in my life that I watched back to back on a flight back from France was “The Judge”. I’ve probably watched it over a hundred times.

What’s interesting is this past Saturday we were at the Palm Beach celebrating my wife’s birthday and decided to go to the breakers, where 6 years ago Robert and I had a lengthy conversation about his impact in theater around the world.
